supply_chain.dependencies: The Company relies on third-party data centers, primarily Amazon Web Services (AWS), and its own colocation data centers to host and operate its Falcon platform. The Company relies on a limited number of suppliers for several components of the equipment used to operate its cloud platform.
supply_chain.risks: Supply chain disruptions could delay the Company's ability to expand or increase the capacity of its global data center network, replace defective equipment in existing data centers and impact operating costs. The technology industry has experienced widespread component shortages and delivery delays, including as a result of geopolitical tensions, public health crises and natural disasters.

regulatory_risks: Compliance with stringent, complex and evolving laws, rules, regulations and standards relating to data privacy and security in many jurisdictions
Federal Trade Commission regulations on unfair or deceptive acts or practices
Electronic Communication Privacy Act
Computer Fraud and Abuse Act
Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act
Gramm Leach Bliley Act
California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act
Comprehensive state-level data privacy and security laws in numerous U.S. states
Data breach notification laws in all 50 U.S. states
European Union General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)
U.K. GDPR
Data transfer restrictions from EEA and U.K. to third countries
EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework and U.K.-U.S. Data Bridge adequacy decisions
Federal Risk and Authorization Management Program (FedRAMP) certification requirements
Department of Defense Impact Level 5 provisional authorization
Government certification, software supply chain, or source code transparency requirements
Export control laws and U.S. economic sanctions laws (U.S. Commerce Department's Export Administration Regulations, U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control)
Import and licensing requirements in various countries for encryption technology
U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA)
U.K. Bribery Act 2010
Anti-corruption, anti-bribery, anti-money laundering laws in multiple jurisdictions
Employment and labor laws
Workplace safety regulations
Product safety regulations
Environmental laws
Consumer protection laws
Import and export controls
Federal securities laws
Tax laws and regulations
AI-related legislation and regulation (including EU AI Act, California AI laws)
Critical infrastructure software resiliency and concentration risk regulations
SEC cybersecurity disclosure requirements
OECD Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS) Pillar Two global minimum tax
Digital Services Taxes (DST) in various countries

sanction_risks: U.S. embargoed or sanctioned countries, governments, persons and entities restrictions
Prohibitions on sale or supply of certain products and services to U.S. embargoed or sanctioned countries
Economic and trade sanctions regulations maintained by U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control
Geopolitical tensions may intensify cyberattacks against the Company as a cybersecurity provider
